definition: Via the Kalecki profit equation (Profits = Investment + Government Deficit + Net Exports - Household Saving), the federal deficit (~$1.8-2T, 6-7% GDP) is the dominant profit sustainer, while AI capex (~$250B) is the marginal contributor — approximately the entire growth margin of US private investment. A 25% capex cut (~$60-75B) mechanically reduces aggregate profits by a comparable amount, transforming AI capex from a sector story to a macro story.
evidence: Kalecki profit equation is an accounting identity
Federal deficit ~$1.8-2T (6-7% GDP) is dominant profit sustainer
AI capex ~$250B is approximately the entire growth margin of US private investment
25% capex cut (~$60-75B) mechanically reduces aggregate profits by comparable amount
open_questions: If fiscal consolidation reduces the deficit floor, does the macro sensitivity to AI capex increase or decrease?
Is the Kalecki channel strong enough to propagate a hyperscaler capex cut into a recession?
How does the DOGE fiscal paradox (consolidation as dollar-negative) interact with the Kalecki mechanism?
